title: Cosmos atrosanguineus
taxonomy: Cosmos atrosanguineus is classified within the family Asteraceae. This family includes a wide variety of flowering plants commonly known as daisies or sunflowers. The genus Cosmos is a group of annual or perennial plants native to the Americas. They are known for their bright, showy flowers that bloom in shades of pink, purple, and red, and sometimes white. As a member of the Asteraceae family, Cosmos atrosanguineus is closely related to other flowering plants in this family.
uses: Cosmos atrosanguineus, also known as the Blackseed Cosmos, has been traditionally used in folk medicine, but its effectiveness and safety for human consumption have not been extensively scientifically studied. intro: The plant Cosmos atrosanguineus, also known as Mexican Aster or Bloodflower, is a flowering perennial plant native to Mexico and parts of Central America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which can aid in reducing inflammation and fighting off infections. The bioactive constituents of Cosmos atrosanguineus include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Cosmos atrosanguineus is often made into a tea, tincture, or infusion, and is commonly used to treat digestive issues, fever, and respiratory problems.
